# Phase 10 Constitutional Consolidation Plan

Phase: 10.0.36 - Constitutional Civilization Stability Consolidation, Final Governance Coherence & Survivability Integration Doctrine  
Status: Planning-only. Runtime remains disabled.

## Objective

Consolidate doctrine coherence, survivability integration, legitimacy continuity, interoperability, boundary reconciliation, and anti-contradiction safeguards without creating centralized authority or runtime execution.

## Scope

- Constitutional coherence doctrine.
- Governance integration framework.
- Survivability consolidation philosophy.
- Doctrine interoperability standards.
- Anti-fragmentation integration doctrine.
- Constitutional consistency verification.
- Long-horizon governance coherence.
- Doctrine boundary reconciliation.
- Integrated legitimacy continuity.
- Constitutional harmony standards.
- Civilization stabilization.
- Anti-contradiction survivability.

## Non-Scope

- Runtime enablement.
- APIs, providers, adapters, credentials, or OAuth.
- Prisma, Supabase, Twilio, email, or background jobs.
- Autonomous constitutional consolidation systems.
- Automatic governance harmonization.
- Self-reconciling authority systems.
- Centralized constitutional authority.
- Repository mutation systems.
- Rollback execution.
- Deployment authority.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Planning docs remain isolated under governance documentation paths.
- Doctrine scope, non-scope, lineage, and conflicts remain visible.
- Integration preserves legitimacy, evidence, dissent, finite authority, and governance/runtime separation.
- Contradictions fail closed into human constitutional review.
- Consolidation does not centralize authority.
- Runtime and deployment authority remain prohibited.
